body:
Bead: ddx-b8926c2d — agent: final sweep — runtimelint passes against the tree
Stage: SD-024 Stage 4
Result: NO CHANGES (prerequisite work not actually done)

(a) What is done
- Read SD-024 §"Stage 4 — Lint enforcement" and TD-024 §"Lint rule (Stage 4)"
  to confirm the bead's expectation: run
  `cd cli && go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...`
  and assert exit 0 against a post-cleanup tree.
- Verified the analyzer is missing:
    `find . -iname '*runtimelint*'` → no matches.
    No file in cli/tools/lint/ named runtimelint; only evidencelint
    exists at cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/.
    `go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...` →
    "stat .../tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint: directory not
    found" (exit 1).
- Inspected the dependency bead ddx-880b7d8b ("tools/lint: runtimelint
  analyzer + Lefthook + CI wiring"). It is marked status=closed in the
  tracker (commit e9f2d53f), but its event body in the tracker shows
  the previous attempt itself returned a NO CHANGES rationale citing
  blocked prerequisite work. The analyzer was never actually written
  in the tree; the bead was closed without its acceptance criteria
  being satisfied.
- Verified the upstream legacy-type retirements (Stages 1–3) are also
  incomplete:
    `grep -rn "type RunOptions\|type ExecuteBeadOptions\|type
     CompareOptions\|type QuorumOptions" cli/internal/agent/` →
    cli/internal/agent/types.go:36:type RunOptions struct {
    cli/internal/agent/types.go:81:type QuorumOptions struct {
    cli/internal/agent/types.go:171:type CompareOptions struct {
  TD-024's Stage 4 lint rule pattern (2)/(3) flags any composite
  literal or function parameter of these names. They are still
  declared and used throughout the tree, so even if runtimelint
  existed it would emit a large number of legitimate violations.

(b) What is blocking
1. The runtimelint analyzer source does not exist. The acceptance
   criterion ("go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...
   exits 0") cannot be evaluated against a non-existent program.
2. The Stage 1/2/3 retirements (beads 16, 22, 28 per TD-024) have not
   landed: ExecuteBeadLoopOptions, RunOptions, ExecuteBeadOptions,
   CompareOptions, QuorumOptions are all still declared in
   cli/internal/agent/types.go and constructed from many call sites.
   The lint by design fails on those.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
- The dependency bead ddx-880b7d8b must actually produce the analyzer
  at cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/ (analyzer.go + cmd/runtimelint/main.go
  + analyzer_test.go + testdata) following the FEAT-022 evidencelint
  pattern, with the closed forbidden-name list and exemptions
  (NoReview, PollInterval, SessionLogDirOverride) per TD-024 §"Lint
  rule (Stage 4)".
- Stages 1, 2, 3 of SD-024 must actually retire ExecuteBeadLoopOptions,
  RunOptions, ExecuteBeadOptions, CompareOptions, QuorumOptions and
  migrate every constructor + signature site to *Runtime. The TD-024
  §"Stage ordering" section enumerates the inventory.
- Only then is this final-sweep bead meaningful. Until then, the
  acceptance test would either crash on the missing program or report
  many real violations from the un-retired legacy types — neither
  outcome is "exit 0".

This bead intentionally adds nothing. The right next step is to
re-open ddx-880b7d8b (and its own deps 16/22/28) and complete them
before retrying ddx-b8926c2d.

Bead: ddx-b8926c2d — agent: final sweep — runtimelint passes against the tree
Stage: SD-024 Stage 4 (final sweep)
Result: NO CHANGES (prerequisite Stage 1–3 retirements not actually landed)

(a) What is done
- Confirmed the runtimelint analyzer is now present and runnable
  (B29 / ddx-880b7d8b shipped in commit 40ac58bb):
    cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go
    cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint/
    cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer_test.go
    cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/testdata/
- Ran the acceptance command:
    cd cli && go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...
  → exit 3, 88 violations across 29 files.
- Cross-checked the analyzer's closed forbidden list (legacyOptionsTypes
  in tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go) against current source:
    grep '^type \(RunOptions\|QuorumOptions\|CompareOptions\) ' \
         cli/internal/agent/types.go
  → still declared at types.go:36, types.go:81, types.go:171.
  ExecuteBeadOptions and ExecuteBeadLoopOptions appear retired
  (no declarations found), but their replacement Runtime structs
  are not adopted at every call site — many *_test.go files and
  several production files still construct or accept the legacy
  options types.

(b) What is blocking
1. Stage 2 retirement bead ddx-ade5ebb3 ("agent: retire RunOptions +
   CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run") is marked closed in
   the tracker, but the types are still present at
   cli/internal/agent/types.go lines 36, 81, 171, and they are
   constructed in 88 sites across 29 files including production code:
     cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
     cli/internal/agent/runner.go            (8 sites)
     cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go   (10 sites)
     cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go     (4 sites)
     cli/internal/agent/service_run.go       (4 sites)
     cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go
     cli/internal/agent/virtual.go, models.go, grade.go, script.go,
       agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, routing_metrics.go,
       session_index.go
     cli/internal/exec/store.go
     cli/internal/server/server.go           (2 sites)
   And many *_test.go files in cli/internal/agent/ also still use
   the legacy composites/parameters.
2. The acceptance criterion ("exits 0") is impossible to satisfy
   without retiring those types and migrating every call site to the
   *Runtime + ResolvedConfig pair — work that is the substance of
   Stage 2 (ddx-ade5ebb3) and Stage 3 (ddx-a8eaef0a) retirement
   beads, plus their many test-migration siblings. That work is
   far outside the single-bead scope of this final sweep.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
- Re-open ddx-ade5ebb3 and the Stage 3 retirement/migration beads and
  actually perform the migrations: delete the type declarations in
  cli/internal/agent/types.go, port the 28 production call sites and
  the test sites enumerated by `runtimelint ./...` to the
  *Runtime + ResolvedConfig API. The analyzer's own output is the
  authoritative punch list — all 88 violations must be addressed.
- Once `runtimelint ./...` exits 0 against the tree, retry this
  final-sweep bead. At that point the bead becomes a trivial
  green-light commit asserting the post-cleanup invariant.

This bead intentionally adds nothing because the Stages 1–3 closure
in the tracker does not match the state of the tree. A NO CHANGES
return is the correct signal: the right next step is to reopen and
genuinely complete the upstream retirement beads, not to bulk-edit
89 violations under the cover of a "final sweep".

Bead: ddx-b8926c2d — agent: final sweep — runtimelint passes against the tree
Decision: no_changes (blocked on prerequisite work)

## What is done
- Ran `go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...` against the post-cleanup tree.
- Confirmed the bead's AC (`exits 0`) is NOT met: the lint reports 88 violations and exits with status 3.

## What is blocking
The bead's notes explicitly warn that B22 closed as already_satisfied without actually retiring the legacy options types. The current tree still uses `agent.RunOptions` extensively:

- Composite-literal usages (RunOptions{...}) across production code:
  cli/internal/agent/{compare_adapter,execute_bead,execute_bead_review,grade,runner,service_run}.go
  cli/internal/exec/store.go
  cli/internal/server/server.go
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
- Function parameters typed as the legacy options across ~15 production files
  (agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, compare_adapter.go,
   execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go, models.go, routing_metrics.go,
   runner.go, script.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, virtual.go, ...)
- Same pattern repeated across ~20 test files (agent_test.go, claude_stream_test.go,
  models_test.go, prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go, session_index_test.go,
  virtual_test.go, execute_bead_*_test.go, etc.)

Total: 88 violations. This is exactly the state the bead's "REOPENED 2026-04-26" note
predicts: "runtimelint actually run produces ~80 violations against the tree because
B22 didn't actually retire RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions."

## Why this is not a final-sweep task
The "final sweep" bead is intentionally narrow: rerun the lint and assert green to
catch drift introduced after the migration is done. It is not the bead that performs
the migration. Resolving 88 violations across ~25 production files plus ~20 test files
requires:
  - Threading *Runtime + ResolvedConfig through every harness, service, runner,
    compare adapter, execute_bead, claude_stream, virtual, session_index, models,
    routing_metrics, script, exec/store, server, and cmd/agent_cmd call site.
  - Updating every test fixture that constructs a RunOptions{...}.
  - Removing the legacy types themselves once all references are gone.
That is the body of B22, not a sweep. Doing it inside this bead would silently absorb
the entirety of a separate migration bead and mask the fact that B22 is incomplete —
which is precisely the problem the reopened note flags.

## What a follow-up attempt would need
1. B22 (or a successor) must actually land: migrate all RunOptions/CompareOptions/
   QuorumOptions call sites to *Runtime + config.ResolvedConfig and delete the
   legacy types. The runtimelint output above is an exhaustive checklist of the
   remaining call sites.
2. Once B22 is genuinely green (lint exits 0 on the tree), this final-sweep bead
   becomes a one-liner: re-run the same command and confirm no new drift appeared.

Re-queueing this bead now would just produce another no_changes (or, worse, an
attempt that absorbs B22's scope under the wrong bead id). Recommend keeping it
blocked on B22 until B22 ships for real.
rationale: Bead: ddx-b8926c2d — agent: final sweep — runtimelint passes against the tree
